title: [Question] Problem to start container with special / custom host
body:
Hi,
I have a special and minimal host setup based on busybox with iptables, crun runtime and podman on build running (at the moment) from initrd. 

maybe something is missing, because I get that error message if I try to start a podman container.
```
Error: writing file `/sys/fs/cgroup/libpod_parent/cgroup.subtree_control`: No such file or directory: OCI runtime command not found error
```

cgroup v2 is mounted
```
/ # ls -lh /sys/fs/cgroup/
total 0      
-r--r--r--    1 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:19 cgroup.controllers
-rw-r--r--    1 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:20 cgroup.max.depth
-rw-r--r--    1 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:20 cgroup.max.descendants
-rw-r--r--    1 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:20 cgroup.procs
-r--r--r--    1 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:20 cgroup.stat
-rw-r--r--    1 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:34 cgroup.subtree_control
-rw-r--r--    1 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:20 cgroup.threads
-rw-r--r--    1 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:20 cpu.pressure
-rw-r--r--    1 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:20 io.pressure
drwxr-xr-x    2 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:19 libpod_parent
drwxr-xr-x    2 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:19 mdevd
-rw-r--r--    1 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:20 memory.pressure
drwxr-xr-x    2 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:19 rngd
drwxr-xr-x    2 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:19 sshd
drwxr-xr-x    2 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:19 udhcpc
```

```
/ # ls -lh /sys/fs/cgroup/libpod_parent/
total 0      
-r--r--r--    1 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:21 cgroup.controllers
-r--r--r--    1 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:21 cgroup.events
-rw-r--r--    1 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:21 cgroup.freeze
-rw-r--r--    1 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:21 cgroup.max.depth
-rw-r--r--    1 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:21 cgroup.max.descendants
-rw-r--r--    1 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:21 cgroup.procs
-r--r--r--    1 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:21 cgroup.stat
-rw-r--r--    1 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:19 cgroup.subtree_control
-rw-r--r--    1 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:21 cgroup.threads
-rw-r--r--    1 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:21 cgroup.type
-rw-r--r--    1 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:21 cpu.pressure
-r--r--r--    1 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:21 cpu.stat
-rw-r--r--    1 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:21 io.pressure
-rw-r--r--    1 0        0              0 Mar  7 13:21 memory.pressure
```

Containers started with crun working fine.
```
/ # crun list
NAME   PID       STATUS   BUNDLE PATH                            
sshd   822       running  /containers/services/sshd              
mdevd  826       running  /containers/services/mdevd             
rngd   827       running  /containers/services/rngd              
udhcpc 829       running  /containers/services/udhcpc 
```

I know it isn't easy because of the very special host setup, but maybe someone know on what that error message depends?
```
Error: writing file `/sys/fs/cgroup/libpod_parent/cgroup.subtree_control`: No such file or directory: OCI runtime command not found error
```

Any hint / idea what could be missing here?

comments.body:
You mention containers started with crun - what do you mean by that? Are you manually crafting OCI specs and passing them in? If so, can you provide one of those, so we can see cgroup configuration in the spec?

@giuseppe PTAL

Sorry, my bad...

Running container with crun works fine with my setup. Podman just try to start an alpine image like that
```
podman run --rm -ti alpine sh
```

Used default libpod config with runtime changed to crun and `no_pivot_root = true`.
Host use busybox init. So no systemd, firewalld or something else.

Any hint where to search for my error message? Missing kernel module? Missing libraries / dependencies?
@pwFoo did you find an answer? Running into this as well
